---
title: "Asymptotic Dist. of MLE"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
format: html
---

Let $X_{1},\ldots,X_{n} | \theta \overset{iid}{\sim} \text{Geom}(\theta)$. It can be shown that $\hat{\theta}_{MLE} = \frac{1}{\bar{X} + 1}$.

We found in class that $I_{n}(\theta) = n I(\theta) = \frac{n}{\theta^2 (1-\theta)}$. Thus, 
$$\hat{\theta}_{MLE} \overset{\cdot}{\sim} N \left(\theta, \frac{1}{I_{n}(\theta) } = \frac{\theta^2 (1-\theta)}{n} \right)$$

Let's see this in action!

```{r message = F, warning = F}
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(311)
theta_true <- 0.5
fisher_info <- 1/(theta_true^2 * (1-theta_true))
n_vec <- c(5, 10, 100, 1000)
B <- 1000
plot_ls <- list()
for(l in 1:length(n_vec)) {
  mle_mean <- theta_true
  mle_var <- 1/ ( n_vec[l]*fisher_info)
  mle_sd <- sqrt(mle_var)
  mle_vec <- replicate(1000, 1/(mean(rgeom(n_vec[l], theta_true)) + 1 ))
  plot_ls[[l]] <- data.frame(mle = mle_vec, n = n_vec[l]) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = mle))+
    geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)))  +
    stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = mle_mean, sd = mle_sd), col = "orange") +
    xlim(c(mle_mean - 4 * mle_sd,mle_mean + 4 * mle_sd)) +
    labs(title = paste0("n = ", n_vec[l])) +
    theme(text = element_text(size = 16))
}
gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = plot_ls)


```
